ddls:
CREATE TABLE "instructions" (
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each instruction */,
  "mnemonic" TEXT /* Mnemonic for the instruction */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the instruction (e.g., arith, logical, control flow) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the instruction */,
  "isa_version" TEXT /* Version of the ISA this instruction belongs to */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the instruction was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operands" (
  "operand_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operand */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction using the operand */,
  "operand_type" TEXT /* Type of the operand (e.g., S, U, F, I) */,
  "operand_width" INTEGER /* Width of the operand (in bits) */,
  "index" INTEGER /* Index of the operand in the instruction */,
  "register" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the operand is a register */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operand_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operands_operand_type FOREIGN KEY ("operand_type") REFERENCES operand_types ("operand_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operands_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operand_types" (
  "operand_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operand type */,
  "operand_type" TEXT /* Name of the operand type (e.g., S, U, F, I) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the operand type */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the operand type (e.g., binary, decimal, hexadecimal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operand_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category" TEXT /* Name of the category (e.g., arith, logical, control flow) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category" INTEGER /* Parent category ID (for hierarchical categorization) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "execution_modes" (
  "mode_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mode */,
  "mode" TEXT /* Name of the mode (e.g., scalar, vector) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the mode */,
  "isa_version" TEXT /* Version of the ISA this mode belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("mode_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operand_widths" (
  "width_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each width */,
  "width" INTEGER /* Width of the operand (in bits) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the width */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value that can be represented by this width */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value that can be represented by this width */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("width_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "execution_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction being executed */,
  "operand_values" TEXT /* Values of the operands used */,
  "result_value" TEXT /* Result of the instruction execution */,
  "cycles_taken" INTEGER /* Number of cycles taken to execute the instruction */,
  "execution_mode" INTEGER /* Mode of execution used */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_execution_results_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., architect, engineer, admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date when the user last logged in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the simulator */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the simulator was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction being versioned */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the instruction */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was released */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_versions_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operand_value_types" (
  "value_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each value type */,
  "value_type" TEXT /* Name of the value type (e.g., integer, float, immediate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the value type */,
  "format" TEXT /* Format of the value type (e.g., binary, decimal, hexadecimal) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_operand_values" (
  "operand_value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operand value */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction using the operand */,
  "operand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operand */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the operand */,
  "value_type" INTEGER /* Type of the value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operand_value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_operand_values_operand_id FOREIGN KEY ("operand_id") REFERENCES operands ("operand_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_instruction_operand_values_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "operand_value_types_used" (
  "value_type_used_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each value type used */,
  "operand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operand using the value type */,
  "value_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the value type used */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of usage of this value type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_type_used_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operand_value_types_used_value_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("value_type_id") REFERENCES operand_value_types ("value_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_operand_value_types_used_operand_id FOREIGN KEY ("operand_id") REFERENCES operands ("operand_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_execution_types" (
  "execution_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each execution type */,
  "execution_type" TEXT /* Name of the execution type (e.g., scalar, vector) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the execution type */,
  "isa_version" TEXT /* Version of the ISA this execution type belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("execution_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "instruction_operand_execution_types" (
  "operand_execution_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each operand execution type */,
  "operand_id" INTEGER /* ID of the operand using the execution type */,
  "execution_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the execution type used */,
  "frequency" INTEGER /* Frequency of usage of this execution type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operand_execution_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "hazards" (
  "hazard_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hazard */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction that encountered the hazard */,
  "hazard_type" TEXT /* Type of hazard (e.g., data, control, structural) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the hazard */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hazard_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_hazards_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "stalls" (
  "stall_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each stall */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction that encountered the stall */,
  "stall_type" TEXT /* Type of stall (e.g., pipeline, data, control) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the stall */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stall_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_stalls_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "instruction_id" INTEGER /* ID of the instruction being measured */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., IPC, CPI, throughput) */,
  "metric_value" TEXT /* Value of the performance metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_instruction_id FOREIGN KEY ("instruction_id") REFERENCES instructions ("instruction_id")
)
